steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Both terms share $34$. Factor it out:$$34x+34y=34(x+y)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $34(x+y)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Since $34$ is a common factor of each term in the given expression, the expression can be rewritten as $34\left(x+y\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $34y+34y$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This expression is equivalent to $34x+34x$.
